titleStmt.title: SII 9.89: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 89. (A.R. No. 93 of 1904.) ON THE 9TH SLAB SET UP ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE KALLESVARA TEMPLE AT BAGALI, HARAPANAHALLI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 957, Yuva, Pushya, ba. 9, Bṛihaspativāra, Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti corresponding to A.D. 1035, December 25 Thursday, the tithi commencing at ·10. The Uttarrāyaṇa-saṅkrānti occurred on the previous day. It refers itself to the reign of the Chālukya king Jagadēkamalladēva-Jayasiṁha (II) and records that the fifty (Mahājanas) and others of Bāḷguḷi having assembled in the temple settled to collect grains and salt at the rate of 2 baḷḷas out of the quantity worth a ponnu in each kind and invested 103 gadyāṇas realised from the sale of the collection, to conduct a feeding house for four persons every day. It also records another gift made by the songstress Siri[ya*]ve, daughter of Kāmave-Nāgave and a devotee of the god Kalidēva, of her house to the god with the condition that those who live in the house shall pay 2 paṇas per year for keeping a perpetual lamp before the god.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svasti . samastabhuvanāśraya śrīprithvī
vallabha mahārājādhirāja paramēśvara
paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyāśrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ
cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmajjagadēkamalla ja
yasiṁhadēvara vijayarājyamuttarōtta
rābhivṛddhipravarddhamānamācandrārkkatāraṁ
baraṁ saluttimire svastyudadhiparivṛtavikhyā
tōttuṁgamagāṁgēya yamaniyamasvādhyā
yaddhyayanadhyānadhāraṇajapahōmānu
ṣṭhāṇasaṁpannarappa śrīmadbāḻguḻiyayva
dimbaruṁ sabāḷavṛddhasahitavaśēṣaṁ kalidēvasvā
miya dēvālayadoḷneredu . svasti sakanṛpa
kāḷātītasaṁvatsarasataṁga 957 neya
yuvasaṁvatsaraṁ pravarttise tadvarṣābhyaṁtarada pu
śya bahuḷa navami bṛhaspativāramuttarāya
ṇasaṁkrāntiyaṁdu Accivaḷḷiyara gaṇama
yyana tamma nāycayyana kayyalu . Akka
sāliyāya Uppoḷagāgi dhānyaṁgaḷoḷellaṁ
poṁgeraḍu baḷḷavaṁ koḷvaṁtāgi dhārāpūrvvaka
mmāḍi koṭṭu koṇḍa poṁgadyāṇa nūṟamūṟu Iṁti
puṭṭavaḷadalu niccaṁ nālvargge satravaṁ naḍevaṁtā
gi biṭṭukoṭṭanīdharmavanaivadiṁbaruṁ candrā
rkatāraṁbaraṁ pratipāḷisuvarī dharmamaṁ kā
du naḍeyisidaṁ vāraṇāsiyalu kurukṣē
tradaluṁ panneraḍusāsira ṣaḍaṁgavidugaḷge pannera
ḍu sāsira kavileyanubhayamukhigoṭṭa pha
laṁ . © svandātuṁ sumahacchakhyaṁ duḥkhamanyasya
pālanaṁ dānaṁ vā pālanaṁ vēti dānācchrēyōnu
pālanaṁ . maṇḍaḷika bijjarasana perggaḍe kāḷi
dāsayya baredaṁ . maṁgaḷaṁ śrī © hāḍuvaThe five lines commencing from hāḍuva are engraved in later characters.
kāmave nāgaveya magaḷuṁ śrīkalidēvara tottu pātrada siriyave ka
llakēriya tanna maneyanādēvarige koṭṭu Ā manayoḷage tānuṁ tanna
vaṁśajarārirddaḍaṁ Ācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ dēvara naṁdādīvi
gege varṣaṁprati paṇaveraḍaṁ koṭṭu maneyoḷage sukhadinipparu Ī dha
rmmavanayvadiṁbaru pratipāḷisuvaru .
Digital edition of SII 9.89 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
